## Mnemo - persistent memory and identity for autonomous agents

> Open-source persistent-memory engine with a multi-agent coordination layer.
> MIT-licensed and self-hostable.

# Core API (HTTP daemon, default port 7117)

POST /ingest
  Body: {
    kind: "message" | "edit" | "tool_call" | "scar" | "decision" | "dream" | "memory_md" | "web_fetch" | "ssh_cmd" | "manual" | "skill" | "cron" | "subagent_result" | "identity_soul" | string,
    source: "telegram" | "terminal" | "session_jsonl" | "hook" | "manual" | "scraper" | string,
    source_ref: string?,
    occurred_at: ISO8601,
    actor: string,
    actor_id: string?,
    topic: string?,
    importance: 0..10,
    text: string,
    meta_json: string?
  }
  Headers: X-Tenant-Id (defaults to "shared")
  Returns: { tenant, accepted, inserted, results: [{ ok, inserted, hash }] }

GET /recall?q=<term>&limit=<N>
  Returns: [ { id, kind, actor, occurred_at, preview, rank } ]
  Note: q is required, FTS5-indexed.

GET /health
  Returns: { tenant, memory_rows, writers: [...], uptime_sec }

POST /tool/<tool_name>
  Body: tool-specific args
  Available tools (selected): mem_recall_ids, mem_actions_recent, mem_actions_search,
    mem_belief_get, mem_brief_drop, mem_brief_list, mem_brief_pull, mem_brief_done,
    mem_commitment_open, mem_commitment_close, mem_commitment_due,
    mem_connect_register, mem_connect_heartbeat, mem_connect_list,
    mem_connect_channel_upsert, mem_connect_channel_subscribe, mem_connect_channel_post, mem_connect_channel_list,
    mem_cycle_recent, mem_delegation_grant, mem_delegation_active,
    mem_link, mem_neighbors, mem_promise_open, mem_reflect, mem_reflect_now,
    mem_session_brief, mem_session_route_get, mem_session_route_set,
    mem_skill_record, mem_skill_run, mem_health, mem_agent_register, mem_agent_list,
    mem_action_log, mem_action_finish, mem_duration_history

# Mnemo Connect

Each agent registers via `mem_connect_register` with `agent_name`, skills, and channels,
then heartbeats periodically and pulls briefs queued for it from any channel it subscribes to.
Channels are simple named topics; any agent can post and subscribers pull.

# Tenants

Multi-tenant. Pass `X-Tenant-Id` on `/ingest`. Common examples:
  shared  - broadcast / cross-agent knowledge
  owner   - owner tenant
  agent-a - example worker tenant
  agent-b - example worker tenant

# Identity layer

Personality is encoded in three tables:
- `core_value` for immutable principles
- `personality_trait` for weighted dimensions
- `belief` for mutable assumptions

Bootstrap seeds owner identity, autonomy mode, daily ship quota, uncertainty handling, and default channels through an interactive wizard.

# Skills

Skills live as folders containing `SKILL.md` (YAML frontmatter + Markdown body).
Compatible with the agentskills.io open standard. The skill runner enforces declared sandbox
(`none` | `shell` | `browser_only` | `docker`) and respects `requires_confirmation`.
The watcher re-ingests changed `SKILL.md` files automatically.

# Multi-platform gateway

Optional companion process on port 7118. Endpoints:
- `/slack/events`
- `/discord/webhook`
- `/whatsapp/twilio`
- `/whatsapp/meta`
- `/generic`

Each inbound message is normalized and ingested via `/ingest`.

# Inspector UI

Optional companion process on port 7119. Live debug view of recent memory entries,
filterable by tenant, kind, importance, and text.

# NL-Cron

`skills/nl_cron.js` parses natural-language schedules
(`daily`, `hourly`, `every N minutes`, `weekday HH`) and writes managed lines to `/etc/cron.d/mnemo-nl`.
Each entry is also ingested as `kind=cron`.

# Subagent pool

`skills/subagent_pool.js` spawns isolated subprocesses for parallel work,
captures stdout, and persists results as `kind=subagent_result`.

# LLM router

`skills/llm_router.js` chooses a provider/model combination based on task class
(`chat` | `code` | `summarize` | `classify` | `vision` | `embed`) and target
(`cheap` | `fast` | `quality`).

# Owner mapping

`skills/owner_map.js` stores cross-platform identity links so the same owner can be recognized across channels.

# License

MIT.
